blurb: Supports Alberta's municipal governance, property assessment, building safety standards, and community infrastructure — providing oversight of municipal finances, administering safety codes, ensuring accessible libraries, and supporting disaster recovery programs.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Albertans can participate in effective and accountable municipal governance
goals.description: The ministry supports effective municipal governance, provides oversight of municipal finances and accountability, manages provincial property assessment standards, and ensures property owners have access to fair and transparent assessment processes.

foundational_services.name: Set standards for municipal governance, accountability, and property assessment
foundational_services.desc: Establishes and enforces standards for municipal government operations, financial accountability, democratic processes, and property assessment practices across Alberta municipalities. Provides oversight and guidance to ensure municipalities meet provincial governance standards.
foundational_services.users: Municipal councils, municipal staff, property assessment professionals

main_services.name: Participate in or understand municipal governance

supporting.name: Find information about your municipality's finances and services
supporting.desc: Albertans can access information about their municipality's financial reports, budgets, service offerings, and governance structure. Municipal financial data and service information is available through municipal websites and provincial reporting platforms.
supporting.users: Property owners, residents, municipal electors, taxpayers

reach_estimate.estimate: Millions of Albertans access municipal information annually
reach_estimate.basis: All property owners and residents in Alberta are affected by municipal governance and services

economic_impact.note: Access to municipal financial and service information supports informed civic participation and transparency

supporting.name: Understand or participate in a municipal election
supporting.desc: Albertans can find information about municipal elections, voting procedures, candidate information, and electoral timelines. The ministry supports municipal election administration and provides guidance on voting eligibility and participation.
supporting.users: Voters, candidates, municipal election officials, electors

reach_estimate.estimate: Millions of Albertans participate in municipal elections
reach_estimate.basis: All Alberta residents are eligible to vote in municipal elections in their jurisdiction

economic_impact.note: Fair and accessible elections are fundamental to municipal democracy and community accountability

supporting.name: Appeal a municipal planning or development decision
supporting.desc: Property owners and affected residents can appeal municipal planning decisions, development approvals, or land use designations. The appeal process provides an independent review of municipal decisions affecting land development and use.

reach_estimate.basis: Appeals available for significant municipal planning decisions affecting property rights

economic_impact.note: Independent planning appeals provide due process protection and ensure fair land use decisions

main_services.name: Understand or challenge your property assessment

supporting.name: Look up your property assessment
supporting.desc: Property owners can look up their assessed property value, assessment classification, and assessment details through online portals or by contacting their municipal assessment authority. Assessment information is used for property tax calculations and planning purposes.

reach_estimate.estimate: Millions of property owners access assessment information annually
reach_estimate.basis: All properties in Alberta are assessed for tax and planning purposes

economic_impact.note: Transparent assessment information is critical to property tax fairness and property owner confidence

supporting.name: File a complaint about your property assessment
supporting.desc: Property owners who disagree with their assessment can file a complaint or appeal through their municipal assessment review board or through provincial appeals processes. The complaint process allows property owners to challenge assessments they believe are inaccurate.
supporting.users: Property owners, property managers, real estate professionals

reach_estimate.basis: Assessment appeals available to property owners across all Alberta municipalities

economic_impact.note: Accessible assessment appeals protect property owner rights and ensure assessment accuracy

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Alberta buildings meet safety codes and development standards
goals.description: The ministry develops and administers Alberta's safety codes for buildings, electrical systems, gas installations, plumbing, and fire protection. The ministry ensures new construction and renovations meet safety standards and provides dispute resolution and new home protection.

foundational_services.name: Develop and administer safety codes and building standards
foundational_services.desc: Develops and maintains Alberta's safety codes framework for buildings, electrical, gas, plumbing, and fire protection systems. Works with accredited agencies and safety codes authorities to ensure all new construction and major renovations comply with safety standards.
foundational_services.users: Accredited agencies, safety codes authorities, construction industry, municipalities

relationships.service: Apply for a construction or renovation permit
Find your local safety codes authority or accredited agency

main_services.name: Get a building, electrical, gas, plumbing, or fire permit

supporting.name: Apply for a construction or renovation permit
supporting.desc: Property owners and contractors can apply for permits required for new construction, additions, renovations, or alterations to buildings. Permits ensure projects comply with the Alberta Safety Codes and building standards. Applications are submitted to the local municipal authority or accredited agency.

relationships.service: Develop and administer safety codes and building standards

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of permits issued annually
reach_estimate.basis: All new construction and major renovations in Alberta require permits

economic_impact.note: Safety code compliance protects occupant safety and public health in all buildings

supporting.name: Find your local safety codes authority or accredited agency
supporting.desc: Property owners and contractors can find their local safety codes authority or accredited agency responsible for issuing permits and conducting inspections in their area. The ministry maintains a registry of all authorized authorities and agencies.

channels.label: Safety codes authorities and agencies directory

relationships.service: Develop and administer safety codes and building standards

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands access safety codes authority information
reach_estimate.basis: Anyone planning construction or renovation needs to identify the applicable authority

economic_impact.note: Clear identification of permitting authorities enables smooth project initiation and regulatory compliance

main_services.name: Resolve a safety codes issue or get a new home warranty

supporting.name: Appeal a safety codes decision or order
supporting.desc: Builders, property owners, and contractors can appeal safety codes decisions, inspection orders, or compliance requirements. The appeal process provides independent review of safety codes authority determinations.

reach_estimate.basis: Appeals available for significant safety codes determinations affecting project feasibility

economic_impact.note: Appeals mechanism provides due process and protects legitimate project interests from unjust orders

supporting.name: File a claim under the new home warranty program
supporting.desc: Home buyers and owners can file claims for warranty coverage under the new home buyer protection program. Coverage includes structural defects, electrical and plumbing defects, and other major systems. Claims can be filed during the applicable warranty period.
supporting.users: New home buyers, homeowners, home builders

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of new home buyers benefit from warranty protection annually
reach_estimate.basis: Warranty program applies to most new residential construction in Alberta

economic_impact.note: Warranty protection gives new home buyers security and recourse for construction defects

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Albertans have access to library services and disaster recovery support
goals.description: The ministry supports access to public library services across Alberta and coordinates disaster recovery assistance for communities affected by declared disasters. Programs help communities rebuild and residents recover from major emergencies.

supporting.name: Find a public library near you
supporting.desc: Albertans can find public library locations, hours, services, and programs in their community. Libraries provide free access to books, digital resources, computers, programming, and community space. The ministry supports library funding and coordination across the province.
supporting.users: All Albertans, students, families, seniors, community members

reach_estimate.estimate: Millions of Albertans use public libraries annually
reach_estimate.basis: Public libraries serve communities across all of Alberta with free access to information and services

economic_impact.note: Public libraries provide essential community access to information, learning, and digital services

supporting.name: Apply for disaster recovery assistance after a declared disaster
supporting.desc: Residents and businesses affected by declared disasters can apply for financial assistance to recover from damages. Assistance programs provide funding for emergency repairs, temporary accommodation, and recovery expenses when disasters are officially declared.

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of residents receive assistance following major disasters
reach_estimate.basis: Programs activate following declared disasters affecting communities across Alberta

economic_impact.note: Disaster assistance enables rapid community and economic recovery following catastrophic events

supporting.name: Access information on emergency preparedness for your community
supporting.desc: Albertans can access emergency preparedness information, planning guides, and resources to prepare their families and communities for potential disasters. Information includes emergency kits, evacuation procedures, and community preparedness planning.
supporting.users: All Albertans, families, community leaders, businesses

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of Albertans access preparedness information
reach_estimate.basis: Emergency preparedness is relevant to all Albertans in all communities

economic_impact.note: Community preparedness reduces disaster impacts and enables faster recovery response
